diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Food. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: country - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | country | Spain)
Predicate: ingredient - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | ingredient | Almond)
Predicate: region - Example triple: (Ajoblanco | region | Andalusia)
Predicate: division - Example triple: (Almond | division | Flowering plant)
Predicate: family - Example triple: (Almond | family | Rosaceae)
Predicate: order - Example triple: (Almond | order | Rosales)
Predicate: course - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | course | Italian meal structure)
Predicate: dishVariation - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | dishVariation | "Onion, garlic, black pepper, chili")
Predicate: mainIngredient - Example triple: (Amatriciana sauce | mainIngredient | "Tomatoes, guanciale, cheese, olive oil")
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Andalusia | leader | Susana Díaz)
Predicate: alternativeName - Example triple: (Asam pedas | alternativeName | "Asam padeh")
Predicate: creatorOfDish - Example triple: (Asam pedas | creatorOfDish | Malay cuisine)
Predicate: creator - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | creator | "Rutland Arms, Bakewell, in 1820")
Predicate: servingTemperature - Example triple: (Bakewell pudding | servingTemperature | "Warm (freshly baked) or cold")
Predicate: carbohydrate - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | carbohydrate | 18.0 g)
Predicate: fat - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | fat | 4.8 g)
Predicate: year - Example triple: (Barny Cakes | year | 1999)
Predicate: related - Example triple: (Bhajji | related | Pakora)
Predicate: similarDish - Example triple: (Bhajji | similarDish | "Pakora and other fritters made from wheat or corn flour")

Predicate: language - Example triple: (China | language | Standard Chinese)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Derbyshire Dales | isPartOf | Derbyshire)
Predicate: currency - Example triple: (France | currency | Euro)
Predicate: demonym - Example triple: (India | demonym | Indian people)
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Indonesia | capital | Jakarta)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about food into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        # Specific phrasing for known predicates
        if p == "country":
            phrase = f"{s} is from {o}"
        elif p == "ingredient":
            phrase = f"{s} contains {o}"
        elif p == "region":
            phrase = f"{s} is from the {o} region"
        elif p == "division":
            phrase = f"{s} belongs to the {o} division"
        elif p == "family":
            phrase = f"{s} is in the {o} family"
        elif p == "order":
            phrase = f"{s} belongs to the {o} order"
        elif p == "course":
            phrase = f"{s} is served as a {o}"
        elif p == "mainIngredient":
            phrase = f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}"
        elif p == "dishVariation":
            phrase = f"{s} can be varied with {o}"
        elif p == "leader":
            phrase = f"The leader of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "alternativeName":
            phrase = f"{s} is also known as {o}"
        elif p == "creatorOfDish" or p == "creator":
            phrase = f"{s} was created by {o}"
        elif p == "servingTemperature" or p == "served":
            phrase = f"{s} is served {o}"
        elif p == "carbohydrate":
            phrase = f"{s} contains {o} carbohydrates"
        elif p == "fat":
            phrase = f"{s} contains {o} fat"
        elif p == "protein":
            phrase = f"{s} contains {o} protein"
        elif p == "year":
            phrase = f"{s} was introduced in {o}"
        elif p == "related":
            phrase = f"{s} is related to {o}"
        elif p == "similarDish":
            phrase = f"{s} is similar to {o}"
        elif p == "genus":
            phrase = f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}"
        elif p == "language":
            phrase = f"The language of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            phrase = f"{s} is part of {o}"
        elif p == "currency":
            phrase = f"The currency of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "demonym":
            phrase = f"The people of {s} are called {o}"
        elif p == "capital":
            phrase = f"The capital of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "ethnicGroup":
            phrase = f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "foundationPlace":
            phrase = f"{s} was founded in {o}"
        elif p == "officialLanguage":
            phrase = f"The official language of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "leaderTitle":
            phrase = f"The leader title of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "servingSize":
            phrase = f"The serving size of {s} is {o}"
        elif p == "class":
            phrase = f"{s} belongs to the class {o}"
        else:
            phrase = f"{s} {p} {o}"
        phrases.append(phrase)

    # Combine all phrases into a single fluent sentence.
    # Group phrases by their subject and join them with commas;
    # separate different subjects with "and".
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_phrases = defaultdict(list)
    for t, phrase in zip(triples, phrases):
        subject_phrases[t.subject].append(phrase)

    clause_texts = []
    for subj, phs in subject_phrases.items():
        if not phs:
            continue
        # Keep the first phrase as is.
        base = phs[0]
        # Transform subsequent phrases into subordinate clauses.
        others = []
        for p in phs[1:]:
            lowered = p
            # If the phrase starts with the subject, remove it.
            if lowered.lower().startswith(subj.lower()):
                lowered = lowered[len(subj):].lstrip()
            # Lower‑case the first character to make it a continuation.
            if lowered:
                lowered = lowered[0].lower() + lowered[1:]
            others.append(lowered)
        clause = base if not others else f"{base}, " + ", ".join(others)
        clause_texts.append(clause)

    sentence = " and ".join(clause_texts)
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

Creating one complex sentence from all the given triples will award greater score as well.
